Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS H. CASE of Hennelly & Grossfeld LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-7300 appearing for Defendant Emerson Power Transmission Corporation;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EMERSON POWER TRANSMISSION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted based on the component parts doctrine. The answer sufficiently raises that issue. Defendant timely filed and served the motion. Defendant's notice of motion indicates that defendant personally served the motion on January 21, 2015, and provided the required 75-days notice based on the April 7, 2015, hearing date. See CCP 437c. The court issued an order allowing the motion to be heard within 30 days prior to trial. Plaintiff concedes most of defendant's facts and it is undisputed that defendant's bushings and gear reducers are incorporated into a wide variety of products. (UMF 1). Defendant's products are sold to entities without any specification regarding their application. (UMF 4). Defendant had no role in designing, assembling, installing, or maintaining the BAE baggage system or how its parts would be installed. (UMF 17 and 18). Given the above, the components parts doctrine applies to this case. That doctrine "rests on 'a line of cases holding an entity supplying a nondefective raw material or a component part is not strictly liable for defects in the final product over which it had no control.'" Bay Summit Community Assn. v. Shell Oil Co. (1996) 51 Cal.App.4th 762, 772. Plaintiff does not highlight a specific defect with defendant's products and the duty to provide a guard fell on BAE. Tellez-Cordova v. Cambell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co., (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable. There, the court held on appeal from dismissal on demurrer that a strict liability claim against a manufacturer of grinding tools was viable. The court ruled that the grinder was specifically designed to be used with toxic products and toxic dust was released when using the grinder in a foreseeable manner. That same specificity is lacking in this case. COUNSEL FOR EMERSON POWER SHALL SUBMIT A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT TRACKING THE LANGUAGE OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING REGARDING THE ISSUE OF NOTICE. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
